TextENGNew: Even if I turned on the light in this hideout, the mountains of scrapped cars would conveniently block the glow from leaking out.
...I understood that logically, but out of an abundance of caution, I left the light off.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Crouching in this silent hideout without a single light, holding my breath in the darkness, filled me with no small amount of dread.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: Keeping myself busy with something would have been far more distracting.
...But my mind had already grown sluggish, and I lacked even the energy to think about what to do next.
And yet, I didn't have the courage to lie down and recover my strength, either.
...Every so often, I would start at some suspicious sound I thought I heard, straining my eyes in the dark and killing my breath.@k Over and over again.
Ooishi believed me about the Sonozaki family's conspiracy.
...But without hard evidence, he couldn't take any large-scale action.
That evidence would probably mean locating whatever facility they were using to research the parasite,@k ...but I had no idea where such a facility might be.
The Irie Clinic seemed the most plausible candidate, but I had no proof.
They wouldn't conduct their research somewhere obvious enough for even me to suspect.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Most likely, there was some kind of secret underground laboratory, perhaps repurposed from a wartime air-raid shelter.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Come to think of it... there was a building on the grounds of the Furude Shrine that no one was allowed inside.
I believe it was called the ritual storehouse.
According to Miyo-san's research, the Furude Shrine was the center of Oyashiro-sama worship.
And the ritual storehouse, which held the implements for the bizarre Watanagashi festival, apparently carried deep sacred significance.
If I remembered correctly, Watanagashi itself was originally a medical ritual designed to produce vaccines and build immunity against the overly virulent parasite.
If that were the case, then the ritual implements used in Watanagashi would be medical tools.
Which would make the ritual storehouse both the most sacred and the most medically relevant building in the village.@k ...Suspicious.
Could there be a secret research facility hidden inside the ritual storehouse?
...Damn it.@k Not the Irie Clinic—if they were going to raid anywhere, it should be there.
Why hadn't I thought of that when I was on the phone with Ooishi?!
I could only hope Ooishi would figure it out on his own...
Should I go back to the phone booth and tell Ooishi about the ritual storehouse?
Just as I thought that, I heard the sound of a car engine approaching, and I pressed myself deeper into hiding.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: No car should be driving through here at this hour.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: Terrible premonitions flashed through my mind, tightening my nerves...
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: Before long, I spotted what appeared to be headlights near the old dam construction office.
The car that had passed by must have been heading for the office ruins.
...But that place was deserted—nothing left but empty prefab buildings.
There was absolutely no reason for anyone to visit at this hour.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: As I strained my eyes into the darkness, the car's engine cut off and its lights went dark. In their place, I saw two flashlight beams flickering and squirming.
Those lights seemed to move inside the prefab building.
...They must be searching to see if I was hiding in there.
I held my breath and watched them until they left.
From this distance, with only their flickering lights to go by, I couldn't tell who they were.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: Most likely... they were Sonozaki family operatives.
They must have learned I'd left home, and now they were searching the entire village for me...
...It infuriated me that my face chose to itch and my legs demanded stretching at the very moment I needed to stay perfectly still.
The muggy heat had left me damp with sweat, and for a while now the crooks of my elbows and the back of my neck had been unbearably itchy.
changeReason: Old says 'back of my knees'; new says 'crooks of my elbows'--different body parts named, per JPN 肘の裏 (elbows).

TextENGNew: When I scratched, it made an unbelievably loud rasping sound, startling me into stopping.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...Normally scratching a few mosquito bites never made such a spiteful noise!
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: It had to be proof that my nerves were on edge.
...There was considerable distance between us, and I was holding my breath in the darkness.
I could have sneezed and they wouldn't have noticed, let alone heard me scratch.
...And yet I couldn't tolerate even the smallest sound, so I endured the maddening itch all over my body for a while... only to give in and scratch everywhere furiously in the end.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I would have to live like this, hiding and enduring, from now on. If I was already this tense on just the first night, there was no way I could keep it up.
I clearly needed to develop the kind of thick skin that wouldn't flinch at something like this.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Telling myself to relax, I scratched my neck without restraint and endured the discomfort of sweat that clung to my skin and refused to evaporate.
...My hunger was starting to bother me.
Now my stomach was the one making a fuss.
...Though really, feeling hungry was probably a sign that I was finally relaxing.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But eating would make a fair amount of noise...
I decided to hold off until those people searching the office ruins left.
Go away already. Go away.
Once you leave, I can eat.
...Whether my silent willing had borne fruit or not, the two flashlight beams seemed to have stopped searching around the prefab building.
...But perhaps I had willed too hard.
Those lights were now pointing steadily in my direction.
...Had they noticed me?!
No.@k They were walking toward me!
I hastily curled up, pulled the blanket over myself, and strained my ears.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...Before long... I could feel two sets of footsteps crunching closer and closer.
Their steps approached without the slightest hesitation, and I grew terrified that they had already found me.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But there was no way they could have found me...
If they had known about this spot from the start, they wouldn't have wasted time searching the prefab building.@k And if they had spotted me, they wouldn't be walking at such an unhurried pace.
...It was pure coincidence that they were heading this way...
I was hiding under the blanket, so I couldn't see who they were.
I didn't know where they were shining their flashlights, so I couldn't risk peeking out through the window.
All I could do was curl up and pretend to be a stone until their presence faded away.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: They stopped right near my hideout.
...I could hear their conversation.
``...She ain't here.``
``Let's circle around.@k@vS09/00/tepoB17002. You check over there.``
Their footsteps separated, and I could tell they were each patrolling around the mountains of scrap.
...My hideout was just a renovated shell of an abandoned car.
At a glance, it looked like nothing more than another junked vehicle.
...But if anyone peered inside, it would be immediately obvious this was no ordinary wreck.@k If they found me, I'd be completely trapped...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: From their conversation, it was clear they were searching for me.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ...Then... were these two part of a Sonozaki strike team?!
If they found me... they would wring the life out of me on the spot!
I knew firsthand just how well-suited this place was for killing someone.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: After all, I'd managed to kill two people here.
If even I could do it, professionals like them would have no trouble at all!
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Even so, there was nothing I could do right now.
All I could do was pray they would leave.
At that moment, I heard a faint electronic beeping sound.
``...This is Atsushi, reading you loud and clear.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17003. ...Yes.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17004. ...Gogura?!@k@vS09/00/tepoA17005. Yes, understood.``
``What happened?``
``Rena Ryuugu's apparently in Gogura.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17007. Somebody spotted her there.``
``You're kidding me... This is hopeless.@k@vS09/00/tepoB17005. What do we do now?``
``The boss's orders are to catch her and bring her in no matter what.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17009. Even if she's gone to Sapporo or Hakata, we've got no choice but to go after her.``
``That's impossible. We'll never find her.@k@vS09/00/tepoB17007. ...Do we still have to keep looking?``
``...The boss wants Miyo Takano's scrapbooks that Rena Ryuugu has.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17011. He was in quite a state about it, so they must be worth something.``
``So what do we do?``
``We're leaving a stakeout at the Ryuugu house, and everyone else is pulling back.@k@vS09/00/tepoA17013. Let's head back and have some barley tea or something.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Their footsteps grew distant and eventually inaudible... then came the sound of a car engine starting.
Only then did I finally emerge from the blanket and look outside.
I saw the car's headlights swing into a U-turn as it drove away.
Even the engine's rumble dissolved into the silence of the night...

I expelled the burning sigh I had been holding in all at once,@k then scratched furiously at every mosquito bite I had been enduring, wiped away the sweat that had been clinging to my skin, and released all the coughs I had been suppressing.
I had heard their conversation clearly.@k Those two were unmistakably Sonozaki family operatives.
...It matched what Ooishi had told me.
And they had said something even more important.
They had explicitly stated that the ``scrapbooks`` were the objective.
Until today, I had only guessed that they were after me because I had received Miyo-san's scrapbooks. No one had ever actually confirmed it.
...I could have forced myself to believe it was merely my assumption.
But they had said it outright!
Capture Rena Ryuugu.@k Because she has Miyo Takano's scrapbooks—they said it clearly!
They also mentioned leaving a surveillance detail at the Ryuugu residence.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...Of course they would.
If I were in their position, I would absolutely stake out the house.
Going home was out of the question.
...But... in fact, they had said something far more important than any of that.
They said... ``Rena Ryuugu was spotted in Gogura.``
Under normal circumstances, I would have laughed it off as someone mistaking a lookalike for me.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...But the situation was no longer normal.
Miyo-san was already dead, yet she had ``appeared.``
And I was right here, yet I had ``appeared`` in Gogura.
...Of everything written in Miyo-san's scrapbooks... that was the part I least wanted to believe.
They were... apart from the Sonozaki family's machinations... slowly closing in on me...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...Everything was already beginning to surpass the bounds of common sense.
...Should I fight, or should I flee? I couldn't even make that judgment, and I was being devoured where I stood, frozen in place...?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...No, no... I don't want this...
I absolutely refuse to be devoured while standing paralyzed in fear.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: If I can't win, I want to run.
If I can't run, I want to fight.
...I will find happiness, no matter what. Absolutely!@k I won't just sit here and let it end...!
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: But... the enemy ``prophesied`` in Miyo-san's scrapbooks was drawing closer, little by little, corroding me from within.
If I were to vanish unnoticed from this garbage dump...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The version of me that had appeared in Gogura would slip into my life as if nothing had happened.@k ...No one around me would ever know the difference.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: My father, my friends—none of them would notice as I was replaced...
changeReason: New translation omits substantial content present in the original.

TextENGNew: No—what if I simply hadn't noticed yet...@k What if the people close to me had already begun to be replaced...?
Yes.@k ...The Sonozaki family's planned bioterrorism was merely them dancing on the palm of a greater hand.
changeReason: Old says 'only a part of what they are planning' (direct attribution); new says 'merely them dancing on the palm of a greater hand' (metaphorical, different framing of the conspiracy hierarchy).

TextENGNew: A mouse possessed by their kind loses its fear of cats.
As a result, it no longer flees at the sight of one.
...And so it is eaten, allowing them to migrate into the cat's body.
...The mouse is simply being used for their purpose. It doesn't lose its fear of cats in order to help them.
The same applies to the Sonozaki family.
...They believe they act of their own free will... when in truth, they unknowingly serve a greater purpose.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: This is their ``parasitism,`` stretching back to ancient times.@k We are nothing more than a swarm of mice.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: Most mice are stupid and dull. They never notice their existence, and help fulfill their purpose while laboring under the illusion that they live by their own will.
...But very rarely, a mouse with keen instincts is born.
The perceptive mouse realizes it is being used, and sometimes even detects their presence.
...Of course, since no one believes it, this poses no real problem.
Eventually, even the perceptive mouse concludes it was mistaken, and is swallowed up and disappears.
But even more rarely... a mouse appears that can objectively prove what it has discovered.
That mouse was Miyo-san.
To them... this rare mouse was an extremely dangerous existence.
...And so, when such a mouse appears, they resort to unbelievably forceful, brazen measures to eliminate it.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Those bold, forceful methods... are actually ones we're surprisingly familiar with.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...But the dull mice scoff and refuse to believe.
...They don't realize they are being guided toward disbelief, that they are ultimately aiding them.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...They killed Miyo-san.
And... they tried to replace her.
But their mistake was that the real Miyo-san's body—the one they should have disposed of—was found.@k That's why the replacement failed.
changeReason: Old says 'We found the real corpse'; new says 'the body they should have disposed of was found'--old attributes finding to 'we', new is passive. Also new adds 'should have disposed of' implying intent.

TextENGNew: ...Then... they would try to kill me too, and replace me.
Or they would replace the people close to me... in order to get near me.
...Identical on the outside... but utterly different within. An aberrant existence beyond anything we could imagine.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: No... they would appear.@k They were already here.
A version of Miyo-san appeared the day after she died.
And tonight, a version of me appeared in Gogura.
...More would come.
They were pursuing me openly, heedless of appearances.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: More and more... they would appear...
Just then, I heard something descending the slope of scrap.
I crouched low again and peered suspiciously toward the sound.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...A single flashlight beam.@k ...It seemed to be just one person.
...Who?@k ...At this hour...?
The figure was very small... it was definitely not an adult.
A chill raced through my entire body...!
The flashlight beam pointed this way, and the footsteps approached in a straight line.
...Unlike those men from before, these footsteps moved as though the person knew exactly where I was hiding.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...No... it had to be coincidence.
...I should just hide again.@k I can get through this.
I made it through last time.@k ...Hide. Hide...!!
I pulled the blanket over my head and curled up, willing myself to be a stone...
But with merciless inevitability, the footsteps drew closer...
And then... they knocked on the car that served as my hideout,@k@|@y ...tap, tap... as if politely rapping on a door.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: My eyes flew wide open, and I couldn't stop the cold sweat that poured from every inch of my body.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: ...Wanting desperately to believe that sound didn't mean what I thought it did, my mind went blank as I fought against the terror.
But the knocking came again.
...I could feel brightness through the blanket.
...Was I being fully illuminated by a flashlight...?!
Still, I swallowed my fear.
...As long as I don't move, nothing can touch me... I invented that superstition and clung to it, enduring even the slightest tremor.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``......Hee hee hee hee.``
It was unmistakably laughter.
...Mocking laughter—ridiculing me for still cowering as though I hadn't been found.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: But... I still held fast.@k ...Because if I came out from under here...!!
``...What are you so afraid of?@k@vS09/05/170500094. ...Hee hee hee hee.``
``Ugh... Aaaaaaaaahhhh!!!``
I threw off the blanket and came face to face with the owner of that voice.
...It was... Rika-chan.
...It was an impossible encounter, in an impossible place.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: For Rika-chan to appear here alone in the dead of night... was impossible!
...The realization struck me instantly.
This Rika-chan... certainly looked like Rika-chan from every angle,@k ...but I understood that she was a different existence, one that perfectly mimicked Rika-chan's appearance.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Miyo-san, me... and now even Rika-chan...?
So before my fear could crush me, I deliberately opened my mouth with aggression, trying to rouse even a little courage.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``......Is that... Rika-chan...?``
``...Mii.@k@vS09/05/170500096. ...Who else could I possibly look like?``
Rika-chan answered the way the Rika-chan I knew would.
...But it was impossible.
There was absolutely no way she would visit a place like this at this hour...!!
``...I can tell.@k@vS09/02/170201104. ...You're not really Rika-chan, are you?``

``...I'm afraid you can't fool my eyes.@k@vS09/02/170201106. Even if you look exactly like her... you're not Rika-chan.@k@vS09/02/170201107. You're not my friend, Rika Furude.@k@vS09/02/170201108. ...What are you...?!``
Rika stood there looking taken aback for a while.
...She appeared to be acting as though she didn't understand what I was saying.
But... the pause dragged on too long.
If she truly had no idea what I was talking about, she would have long since been unable to bear the silence. ...But she simply remained quiet.
And then... she finally shed her mask.
``...Hee... hee hee hee hee hee.``
She laughed.
...She laughed as though amused—amused that I had seen through a disguise that should have been impossible to penetrate.
``...Who... are you?!!``
She didn't answer my question. ...She just went on laughing to herself for a while.
Then... she looked me straight in the eye—revealing an expression Rika-chan had never once shown—and spoke.

``I've been alive in this world for quite some time.@k@vS09/05/170500101. ...But you're the first one to ever see through me.@k@vS09/05/170500102. I thought you had sharp instincts from the moment we met, but... I'm impressed.@k@vS09/05/170500103. Hee hee hee...``
...She had spoken aloud the very thing I feared most in my heart... I was clearly in a state of shock.
If she says this, it would be terrifying. If she says that, there would be no room for misinterpretation.@k ...It was as though she had read from a list of my deepest fears, voicing them one by one.
changeReason: Different framing: old says 'she told me everything that could scare me'; new describes a list of deepest fears.

TextENGNew: ``Let me go by the name Rika for now.@k@vS09/05/170500105. ...I've been calling myself that for quite a long time, after all.``
``...Oh, really...@k@vS09/02/170201112. ...A long time... How long would that be...?``
``It's nothing remarkable.@k@vS09/05/170500107. Only about a hundred years or so.``
``...Wh... What?!@k@vS09/02/170201114. What are you saying?!!!``
My emotions were contradicting themselves.
I knew the being before me was not Rika, yet I found myself rejecting this non-Rika existence.
...As long as humans harbor this kind of emotion... they will never be able to truly perceive the unknown.
...And that is precisely why humanity cannot recognize ``them.``

``You don't need to be afraid.@k@vS09/05/170500109. You're inside the car.@k@vS09/05/170500110. I'm outside it.@k@vS09/05/170500111. ...It's not easy for either of us to do anything rash to the other.@k@vS09/05/170500112. I'd say this is a conveniently safe distance, wouldn't you?``

``No matter how many centuries I've lived, Rika Furude is still just a small child.@k@vS09/05/170500114. I don't imagine I could overpower you.@k@vS09/05/170500115. ...If anything, I'm the one who should worry about you lunging at me in a rage.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...I realized that this eerie being wearing Rika's face had come here to talk to me, and I recovered a sliver of composure.
But... what could she possibly want to discuss with me, in a place like this, at this hour?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I was already a fish on the cutting board.@k ...In that case, I might as well face this with what dignity I could muster.
I waited to see what she would do... and then she spoke.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ``...You're frightened of something.@k@vS09/05/170500117. ...Am I right?``
Her tone was snide.
...I couldn't believe Rika-chan's adorable face could twist into such a distorted smile.
So I wrestled down the fear welling up inside me and answered firmly.
``...Yes.@k@vS09/02/170201116. Thanks to the likes of you.``
``I see.@k@vS09/05/170500119. ...What is it you're afraid of?``
``...You should know that better than anyone!!``
She had claimed that with one of us in the car and the other outside, we were both protected.
But it was obvious that I was the one being cornered.
...While I was talking to her, wouldn't her minions be surrounding me?@k ...That thought made me strain my ears even harder...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Don't be afraid.@k@vS09/05/170500122. I came here to help you.@k@vS09/05/170500123. ...Hee hee hee.``
``Help me...?@k@vS09/02/170201119. Oh, r-really... And how exactly do you plan to do that...?``

``You've just come down with a little illness, that's all.@k@vS09/05/170500125. A simple medicine will make you feel much better.@k@vS09/05/170500126. ...Would it interest you if I said I've brought that medicine with me?``
``...M-Medicine...?``
The thing that was a dead ringer for Rika reached into its pocket and produced a small case.
She snapped it open and showed me the contents.
...When I saw what was inside, I couldn't help crying out.
``...A s-syringe...?!``
A syringe isn't something an ordinary person carries around.@k Nothing made sense anymore.
...But what Rika-chan held in her hand was undeniably a syringe.@k There was no way to interpret that differently...!
``That's right.@k@vS09/05/170500128. This injection will make you feel better.``
``Make me feel better?!@k@vS09/02/170201123. Ha!!@k@vS09/02/170201124. I know what that is!@k@vS09/02/170201125. That's the drug that killed Tomitake-san!!``
``That was the injection given to Tomitake, yes.@k@vS09/05/170500130. But this is different.@k@vS09/05/170500131. Truly.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: She let out a quiet laugh.
It wasn't the laugh that frightened me.
...It was the fact that she had so casually acknowledged the cause of Tomitake-san's death!
And she had just as casually admitted the direct ``cause`` of the affliction that killed him!!

``...You're planning to make me die the same way he did!@k@vS09/02/170201129. And then... the Rena who appeared in Gogura will replace me, and that'll be that.@k@vS09/02/170201130. ...That's your plan, isn't it?!@k@vS09/02/170201131. I'm not letting you give me that injection!!``
``...Well, I expected you wouldn't want it.@k@vS09/05/170500133. It does look rather suspicious, after all.``
``Of course it does...@k@vS09/02/170201133. ...So what are you going to do?!@k@vS09/02/170201134. ...Gang up on me and force the injection like you did to Tomitake-san?!``
changeReason: Old says 'You're smarter than I thought' but Japanese means 'Of course it does' -- different opening response.

TextENGNew: Tomitake-san's autopsy had revealed clear signs that, moments before his gruesome end, he had been in a violent struggle with multiple assailants.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...Which meant they had forcibly administered the injection against his will.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``...I would like to inject you by force, but... that's never really gone well.@k@vS09/05/170500135. So I've given up on the forceful approach.@k@vS09/05/170500136. ...That's why I'm leaving it to your own free will.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``Don't toy with me!!@k@vS09/02/170201136. Who would ever take that sinister injection?!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...I've agonized over this, you know.@k@vS09/05/170500138. How to coax you into accepting the injection.@k@vS09/05/170500139. Force doesn't work, and I can't get your understanding.@k@vS09/05/170500140. ...So in the end, the only thing left is to let you decide for yourself.``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``That goes without saying!!@k@vS09/02/170201138. Who would fall for such a transparent trap?!``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Well, that's exactly the response I predicted.@k@vS09/05/170500142. I didn't have any expectations, anyway.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: She heaved a deep sigh and made a slightly condescending gesture, smiling bitterly.
...As if to say, I'm giving you a chance to be saved, and you're too much of a lower creature to understand...!
Part of me wanted to take the dare and say fine, inject me then—but it was obvious that was exactly what she wanted.
``Go on back and tell your kind that the injection failed!!@k@vS09/02/170201140. I won't lose!@k@vS09/02/170201141. I'll resist to the very end!!``
``And... even if my chances are less than one percent, I'll seize them and destroy every last one of you!!``
``...I see.@k@vS09/05/170500144. ...Then by all means, do your best.@k@vS09/05/170500145. This world is going to end soon anyway.@k@vS09/05/170500146. ...Hee hee hee hee...``
``That's none of your concern.@k@vS09/05/170500148. A frog in a well has no reason to take interest in what lies beyond it, does it?@k@vS09/05/170500149. Hee hee hee.``
Even now, she was uttering such portentous words...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I desperately tried to fathom what this terrifying being—wearing Rika's face—was trying to say.
``...Wh-What... What are you going to do to me?!!``
``Nothing.@k@vS09/05/170500151. Do as you please.``
...That was an unexpectedly anticlimactic answer.
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: ...As far as I knew, ``do as you please`` meant indifference or release when spoken by an adversary—not an inherently bad thing.
Or... did it carry another meaning I wasn't seeing...?
As I turned the words over two and three times, searching for hidden meanings, she said it again.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``Live however you like.@k@vS09/05/170500153. ...'This' Rena Ryuugu no longer interests me.@k@vS09/05/170500154. I'll see about getting along with the 'next' Rena Ryuugu.``
``...I can only pray that the 'next' Rena Ryuugu doesn't have such annoyingly keen intuition.@k@vS09/05/170500156. ...Hee hee hee hee.``
Her words carried the terrible finality of a judge pronouncing a death sentence.

``I used to scramble to mend every little crack the moment one appeared.@k@vS09/05/170500158. ...But somewhere along the way, I grew tired of it.@k@vS09/05/170500159. ...'This' Hinamizawa no longer interests me.``
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ``I'm going to search for the 'next' Hinamizawa.@k@vS09/05/170500161. ...But you only have 'this' one.@k@vS09/05/170500162. So do your best to survive, for what it's worth.``
I understood that she was telling me one terrifying thing after another.
It was an unmistakable declaration of doom... vividly indicating that a terrible end was fast approaching...
Another version of me had already been prepared.
And when I failed to defy my fate and was consumed by the coming catastrophe... preparations had already been made for that other self to seamlessly take my place.
What humans fear most is for their life to lose all value.
...Nothing renders one's life more worthless than being told there is already a replacement standing by.
It was exactly the same as an indirect death sentence!
The thing resembling Rika snapped the syringe case shut and slipped it back into her pocket.
Then she turned on her heel and began climbing the slope of scrap...
At the top, she turned back one final time... and spoke with an expression so cold it didn't even carry an evil smile.
``Well then.@k@vS09/05/170500164. ...Goodbye, 'this' Rena Ryuugu.@k@vS09/05/170500165. ...I hope the 'next' Rena Ryuugu will get along with me, okay?``
``...Most of the time I do manage to get along with them.@k@vS09/05/170500167. ...Hee hee hee hee!``

``I... I won't allow a 'next' Rena Ryuugu!!@k@vS09/02/170201147. I am me—there's only one of me!!@k@vS09/02/170201148. I won't let things go the way you want!@k@vS09/02/170201149. Never,@k@vS09/02/170201150. ever!!``
Did my scream—little more than a shriek—even reach her?
...Her eyes as she looked at me were terribly cold.@k ...As though I were nothing more than a faded recording playing behind a cathode-ray screen.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ``It's fun to have this kind of conversation once in a while, I suppose.@k@vS09/05/170500169. Hee hee hee... Ha ha ha... Ha ha ha ha... Ahahahahahahaha...``
Her laughter gradually faded into the darkness of the night.
...The being with Rika's face that was not Rika was swallowed by that same darkness and vanished.
All that remained... was me.
Abandoned. Disposable at any moment.
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: Told to struggle as much as I pleased, for all the good it would do.
A spare had already been prepared, and I—whose intuition had been too keen—was no longer needed in ``their`` Hinamizawa.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...When I realized that...@k ...I realized that this garbage dump was a gravestone erected just for me.
Then I came back to my senses and recognized that this place could no longer serve as a hideout.
I should pack my things and relocate as quickly as possible.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: She told me to struggle as much as I liked.@k ...Fine. That's exactly what I'll do, damn it...!!
I had to prepare to move.
I would repack my knapsack and leave this place at once.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: The flashlight's beam was too narrow, so I turned on the desk lamp.
A world of sharp shadows and stark contrasts floated into view.
changeReason: Old says 'light made things show up clearly' but Japanese describes a shadow-world of stark contrasts.

TextENGNew: And when I reached for the knapsack... I froze.
The palms of my hands were smeared with a revolting red liquid.
changeReason: Old says 'palm of my hand' (singular) covered with 'an eerie liquid'; new says 'palms of my hands' (plural) smeared with 'a revolting red liquid'--new explicitly identifies the color as red.

TextENGNew: In the darkness, I had thought I was wiping away sticky, unpleasant sweat.
...But it... hadn't been sweat at all...
Then what was this blood...?
I realized almost immediately.
I had been clawing at the mosquito bites on my neck all this time... and at some point, I had drawn blood.
The instant I became aware of it, the scratched wounds recalled their mingled sensation of pain and itch, and I let out a belated groan.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: ...I felt something squirming repulsively in the wound on my neck, and I scratched at it again, trying to dig out whatever it was.
My hand was slicked with fresh, vivid blood once more.
...I had no mirror here, so I couldn't see it... but I already knew what that squirming was.
They were spreading slowly across my entire body, causing terrible itching in the crooks of my elbows and behind my knees.
...If this were someone's first time, they might think they'd been bitten by some nasty insect.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...But unfortunately... I already knew this itch.
I spat the words out with hatred.@k ...Why now? Why here...?!
There was no doubt... it was that phenomenon.
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: If I took a razor and opened the veins in my arm, a dark-red mass of filthy, writhing things would surely come pouring out.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: But that was impossible. I hadn't received any suspicious injections...?!
But then, it didn't necessarily have to be an injection.
...What if... I had been made to ingest the drug without realizing it...?!
I didn't think it could have been in the food at home, but I used to share lunches with my friends at school.@k ...What if it was slipped into my food then...?!
That fake Rika who visited just now... had shone the flashlight on me.
So she must have seen that I was scratching my throat bloody.
...And yet she showed no urgency... casually declaring that I wouldn't last long.
...That's why she had been wearing that sickeningly smug grin the whole time... She knew... she knew I didn't have much time left...!!
The itching was terrible, but I couldn't scratch. The more I scratched, the worse it would get... and in the end I'd meet the same fate as Tomitake-san!
Damn it, damn it...!!
Was this itching temporary?
Or would it only intensify?
changeReason: Different phrasing conveys a noticeably different meaning.

TextENGNew: Was it fleeting, or was it fatal? I knew nothing—all I could do was endure the unbearable itch that consumed my entire body.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: I wiped the blood from around my neck with a towel, clenched my back teeth, and tried to fight off the urge to claw at myself.
The wound throbbed.
...The desire to surrender to the itch and tear my neck open—all I could do was bite down on the corner of the towel and bear it.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I tried clawing painfully at other parts of my body, hoping the sensation would distract me from the itch, but for all the pain I endured, the effect was negligible.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: In the end, the only strategy I could think of was to close my eyes, pray for the itching to subside, and simply endure.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: After a while of making my mind blank with ragged breathing... the itching seemed to ease just a fraction.
I took one deep breath to steady myself, then quickly gathered my belongings.
The itching was nothing compared to the danger of remaining here.@k I had to leave immediately!
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: As I packed, I could feel it.
The destruction that the fake Rika had spoken of—the end that was coming soon.
...I had assumed I would at least survive until that destruction came... but perhaps even that assumption was arrogant.
Long before that destruction arrived...@k ...I might follow the same path Tomitake-san had walked.
The sweat dripping ceaselessly from my body felt revolting.
...I know.
This isn't sweat.@k ...But for now, I'll tell myself it is...!
I wiped my neck once more with the towel and hurried to evacuate.
